context.story_engine: Noble families of Westeros maneuver for power and survival as political alliances shift and ancient threats return from beyond the Wall

plotlines.name: Ned: Honor
plotlines.goal: serve as Hand while maintaining his moral principles
plotlines.obstacle: corrupt court politics, Lannister schemes, pressure to compromise values

plotlines.name: Daenerys: Power
plotlines.goal: transform from pawn to queen and reclaim her birthright
plotlines.obstacle: abusive brother, foreign culture, magical betrayal
plotlines.stakes: death, permanent exile, loss of dragons

plotlines.name: Jon: Purpose
plotlines.goal: find his place and purpose as a bastard
plotlines.obstacle: bastard status, Night's Watch hierarchy, supernatural threats
plotlines.stakes: meaningless existence, death beyond the Wall

plotlines.name: Robb: War
plotlines.goal: rescue his father and protect the North
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister military power, political alliances, inexperience
plotlines.stakes: father's death, Northern defeat, family destruction

plotlines.name: Tyrion: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive accusations and family politics through wit
plotlines.obstacle: false accusations, hostile captors, family expectations

plotlines.name: Catelyn: Protection
plotlines.goal: protect her children from Lannister threats
plotlines.obstacle: distance from children, political complexity, limited resources

plotlines.name: Cersei: Secrets
plotlines.goal: protect her children and maintain power
plotlines.obstacle: Ned's investigation, Robert's suspicions, political exposure
plotlines.stakes: children's death, loss of power, execution

plotlines.name: Arya: Identity
plotlines.goal: survive and maintain her identity while in danger
plotlines.obstacle: court expectations, political violence, forced disguise
plotlines.stakes: death, loss of self, permanent captivity

events.event: Night's Watch soldiers encounter White Walkers who kill all but one deserter
Ned executes the Night's Watch deserter for abandoning his post
Stark children discover orphaned direwolf pups and adopt them
Hand of the King Jon Arryn dies suddenly in King's Landing
King Robert travels to Winterfell to name Ned as his new Hand
Robert proposes marrying Joffrey to Sansa Stark
Catelyn receives secret letter from Lysa claiming Lannisters murdered Jon Arryn
Bran climbs tower and witnesses Cersei having sex with Jaime
Jaime pushes Bran from the window to protect their secret
Viserys forces Daenerys to marry Khal Drogo for an army
Daenerys receives three fossilized dragon eggs as wedding gifts

episodes.theme: the price of power and the corruption of innocence

interactions.description: all plotlines explore how power corrupts and forces moral compromises
Bran's discovery of the Lannister secret directly threatens Catelyn's children
both Daenerys and Ned are forced into positions of power they didn't seek

events.event: Ned departs for King's Landing as Hand, accompanied by daughters Sansa and Arya
Catelyn remains at Winterfell to care for comatose Bran
An assassin attempts to kill Bran
Catelyn fights off the assassin and Bran's direwolf Summer kills the attacker
Jon Snow travels north to join the Night's Watch, accompanied by Tyrion Lannister
Prince Joffrey threatens Arya's commoner friend
Joffrey has the boy killed
Arya's direwolf Nymeria bites Joffrey in defense
Queen Cersei demands the wolf's death
Arya releases Nymeria into the wild
Cersei orders Sansa's direwolf Lady killed instead
Ser Jorah Mormont befriends Daenerys
Daenerys focuses on learning to please her new husband Drogo

episodes.theme: the cost of power and the vulnerability of innocence

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore how violence against children serves adult political purposes
the Kingsroad incident forces Ned to choose between justice and political necessity while Cersei protects her son
both Daenerys and Arya must adapt to survive in hostile environments that threaten their sense of self

events.event: Ned attends his first Small Council meeting and discovers the realm is deeply in debt and poorly managed
Ned learns Arya received a sword from Jon and aspires to fight
Ned arranges swordsmanship lessons for Arya
Catelyn secretly travels to King's Landing to warn Ned about the assassination attempt
Catelyn is intercepted by childhood friend Petyr Baelish
Bran learns he will never walk again and cannot remember his fall
Jon proves superior to the low-born Night's Watch recruits
Lord Commander Jeor Mormont asks departing Tyrion to request more recruits from the King
Pregnant Daenerys stands up to her abusive brother Viserys, threatening him if he harms her again

episodes.theme: the burden of responsibility and the cost of protecting those we love

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters taking on greater responsibility to protect others—Ned as Hand, Catelyn protecting her children, Daenerys protecting her unborn child
Catelyn's arrival in King's Landing directly connects her protective mission with Ned's political struggles

events.event: Tyrion stops at Winterfell while returning south
Tyrion gives Bran a saddle design allowing him to ride despite his paralysis
Catelyn encounters Tyrion at a roadside inn
Catelyn rallies her father's bannermen to arrest Tyrion for allegedly conspiring to murder Bran
Ned secretly investigates Jon Arryn's death
Ned discovers Gendry, one of King Robert's bastard children
Jon protects awkward recruit Samwell Tarly from abuse by other Night's Watchmen
Viserys grows increasingly frustrated with his newly empowered sister Daenerys
Viserys clashes with Daenerys

episodes.theme: the consequences of taking action to protect what matters most

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters taking protective action—Catelyn protecting Bran, Ned protecting the realm through investigation, Jon protecting Sam
Catelyn's arrest of Tyrion directly merges their plotlines as her protective action becomes his survival crisis
Ned's investigation threatens Cersei's secrets, but neither fully understands the other's position yet

events.event: Varys informs the Small Council that Daenerys Targaryen is pregnant
King Robert proposes assassinating the pregnant Daenerys
Ned opposes the assassination plan, considering Daenerys no threat
Ned resigns as Hand of the King in protest
Catelyn takes Tyrion prisoner
Catelyn brings Tyrion to her sister Lysa Arryn's fortress in the Vale
News of Tyrion's capture reaches King's Landing
Jaime confronts Ned demanding answers about his brother's capture
The confrontation escalates into a fight between Jaime and Ned
One of Jaime's men stabs Ned in the leg from behind
Jaime departs to rescue his brother, leaving Ned wounded

episodes.theme: the cost of moral principles in a corrupt world

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore the tension between doing what's right and protecting those you love
Tyrion's capture directly leads to Jaime confronting Ned, merging their conflicts
Ned's moral stance on Daenerys affects her storyline though she remains unaware of the assassination plot

events.event: King Robert reappoints Ned as Hand and leaves him to govern while hunting
Ned learns that Ser Gregor Clegane has committed atrocities in the Riverlands
Ned sentences the Mountain to death and summons Tywin Lannister to trial
Fearing war, Ned orders Arya and Sansa to return to Winterfell for safety
Ned discovers that Joffrey and his siblings are not Robert's children but were fathered by Jaime Lannister
Bran tests his new saddle but is attacked by wildlings
Robb Stark and Theon Greyjoy rescue Bran and capture the wildling woman Osha
Tyrion demands trial by combat
Sellsword Bronn volunteers as Tyrion's champion
Bronn defeats Ser Vardis Egen, winning Tyrion's freedom
Impatient Viserys threatens to kill Daenerys's unborn child
Khal Drogo executes Viserys by pouring molten gold on his head

episodes.theme: the price of justice and the consequences of crossing lines

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters making decisive moves that will have irreversible consequences
Ned discovers the truth about Joffrey's parentage while Cersei remains unaware of his knowledge
both involve the Stark family preparing for and responding to threats against their children

events.event: Ned confronts Cersei, revealing he knows Jaime fathered her children and will tell Robert
King Robert is mortally wounded by a boar while hunting
On his deathbed, Robert names Ned regent until Joffrey comes of age
Ned writes 'my rightful heir' instead of Joffrey's name in Robert's will
Ned sends word to Stannis Baratheon, Robert's brother and legal heir
After Robert's death, Ned confronts Cersei and Joffrey in the throne room
Littlefinger and the City Watch betray Ned and attack his men
Benjen Stark disappears during a ranging beyond the Wall
Jon and Sam are assigned as stewards
Jon is assigned to serve Lord Commander Mormont personally
Ser Jorah exposes Robert's hired assassin before he can poison Daenerys
Drogo vows to conquer Westeros for his unborn son
Drogo begins marching toward the coast

episodes.theme: the price of honor in a corrupt world

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters choosing between duty and survival
Ned's moral stand directly threatens Cersei's secret, forcing both into crisis
the audience knows Robert's assassination attempt on Daenerys while Ned tries to honor Robert's wishes

events.event: Syrio Forel fights guards sent to arrest Arya
Arya escapes the Red Keep
Sansa is captured
Robb receives news of his father's arrest
Robb mobilizes the Northern armies
Robb leaves Bran to rule Winterfell
Lysa Arryn refuses to join the fight against the Lannisters
Catelyn leaves to join Robb's war camp
Tyrion and Bronn are captured by mountain tribesmen
Tyrion convinces the tribesmen to escort him to Tywin's camp
Tywin recruits the tribesmen to fight the Starks
Jon and Sam encounter a reanimated corpse
The corpse attacks Lord Commander Mormont
Jon destroys the corpse with fire
Drogo's khalasar raids settlements to capture slaves
Drogo is wounded in combat
Joffrey names Tywin as Hand of the King
Sansa begs Joffrey to spare Ned's life
Joffrey agrees to spare Ned if he confesses treason

interactions.description: all characters face choices between survival and principles
Catelyn joins Robb's war effort while Tyrion brings reinforcements to the opposing side
Sansa believes she's saving Ned by appealing to Joffrey, but she's actually giving Cersei leverage

events.event: The Stark army reaches the Twins
Catelyn negotiates with Lord Walder Frey for passage and military support in exchange for future marriages
Lord Commander Mormont gives Jon his family's Valyrian steel sword Longclaw
Drogo's wound becomes infected and he nears death
Daenerys asks enslaved witch Mirri Maz Duur to use blood magic to save Drogo
Daenerys goes into labor
Jorah brings Daenerys into the forbidden tent seeking help
Robb sacrifices 2,000 soldiers as a diversion while his main force captures Jaime Lannister
Ned publicly confesses to treason and acknowledges Joffrey as rightful king
Joffrey orders Ned's execution over the objections of Sansa, Cersei, and his advisors

episodes.theme: the price of honor in a world without mercy

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore sacrifice and the cost of doing what you believe is right
Catelyn's negotiations directly enable Robb's military strategy
Ned's execution happens just as Robb achieves military victory that might have saved him

events.event: The Northern lords declare independence from the Seven Kingdoms
The Northern lords crown Robb Stark as King in the North
Tywin appoints Tyrion as acting Hand while he commands the Lannister armies
Jon attempts to desert the Night's Watch to avenge Ned
Jon's brothers convince him to honor his oath
Mormont assigns Jon to an expedition beyond the Wall to search for Benjen
Yoren smuggles Arya out of King's Landing disguised as a boy
Joffrey announces his intention to marry Sansa despite executing her father
Daenerys's baby is born dead and deformed
Drogo remains in a vegetative state from Mirri's treacherous magic
Daenerys ends Drogo's suffering
Daenerys places her three dragon eggs on Drogo's funeral pyre along with Mirri
Daenerys burns Mirri alive on the pyre
Daenerys walks into the flames ignoring Jorah's pleas
Daenerys emerges unharmed from the ashes with three newly hatched dragons
Daenerys's followers kneel before her as the Mother of Dragons

interactions.description: all three plotlines show characters accepting their destiny and transforming into leaders through sacrifice
both Arya and Jon must disguise or suppress their true selves to survive and find their path
Joffrey's announcement directly threatens Sansa's survival and forces her deeper into captivity
